vulnerability.reference: Deployment in safety-critical settings (e.g., autonomous driving) where spurious visual-text correlations cause misinterpretation.
vulnerability.generated: Reliance on compressed cross-modal task vectors without per-task tuning leaves spurious correlations unchecked, enabling performance degradation.

impact.reference: Passenger harm from unexpected autonomous vehicle behaviors when visual cues are misaligned with instructions.
impact.generated: Erroneous and unreliable multimodal task outputs, causing failures in downstream applications relying on VLM cross-modal transfer.

dose_response.reference: Shared vectors transfer spurious correlations, which in safety-critical deployments without validation leads to passenger harm from unexpected autonomous vehicle behaviors.
dose_response.generated: Shared cross-modal task vectors compressing examples propagate spurious correlations, and when developers deploy VLMs without per-task tuning, unchecked reliance on these vectors degrades performance, leading to erroneous multimodal outputs and downstream failures.
